architecture.summary: SITE (Soft head-selection for ICL-derived Task Embeddings) is a gradient-based method that selects task-relevant attention heads in LLMs to inject task-specific embeddings derived from in-context learning few-shot prompts. The system derives embeddings from intermediate activations during inference and uses soft selection to identify which attention heads are most responsive to task signals, enabling targeted embedding injection without modifying the full model.

biblio.title: Soft Head Selection for Injecting ICL-Derived Task Embeddings

baseline.name: few-shot ICL and prior embedding-based adaptation methods

claims.statement: SITE significantly outperforms prior embedding-based adaptation methods and few-shot ICL across various types of open-ended generation, reasoning, and natural language understanding tasks
SITE uses substantially fewer trainable parameters than PEFT
Experiments on 12 LLMs ranging from 4B to 70B parameters demonstrate the generality of the approach
Intra-task and inter-task activation patching analyses reveal strong task dependence in attention head functionality
Prior ICL-driven embedding-based adaptation has not demonstrated consistent performance gains over PEFT or ICL, and its empirical advantages have been limited in practice
